content: GitHub README image hosting options: (A) Checked into repo under screenshots/ or docs/ — simplest, adds binary weight to git history, works fine for <10 PNGs. (B) Orphan 'assets' branch — keeps code branch clean, can wipe+replace images without polluting history; cross-ref via raw URL. (C) GitHub CDN — upload via issue/PR comment drag-drop, generates CDN URL like https://github.com/user/repo/assets/...; zero git weight, survives repo deletion or rename issues. (D) Git LFS — handles large binaries but requires LFS enabled on both server and client; overkill for screenshots. (E) Inline SVG committed to repo — best for animated demos; renders directly on GitHub without external deps, survives offline, zero bandwidth vs GIF. Recommendation for claudebar: SVG animation committed alongside repo (screenshots/animated.svg), static PNG strips also committed (screenshots/strip-*.png). No LFS needed.
